sections.paragraph:
πόρρω adv. (the att. form [=πόρσω, cp. πρόσω in the same sense]; Pla., X.+; LXX; En; EpArist 31; Just., D. 10, 2; Tat.—Thackeray p. 123; B-D-F §34, 2) pert. to a position that is relatively distant, far (away).

ⓐ used as an adv. 1 Cl 3:4; Hv 3, 6, 1. π. εἶναι be far away (B-D-F §434, 1; Rob. 546) Lk 14:32. π. ἀπό (En 32:4; Jos., Vi. 281): π. γίνεσθαι ἀπό τινος be or remain far from someone or someth. fig. (cp. Bar 3:21) 1 Cl 23:3; 30:3 (some would put in b); 39:9 (Job 5:4). π. ἀπέχειν ἀπό τινος be far removed fr. someone, fig. Mt 15:8; Mk 7:6; 1 Cl 15:2; 2 Cl 3:5 (all four Is 29:13).

ⓑ used as a prep. w. gen. (Isocr., Ep. 6, 13 κινδύνων π.; Περὶ ὕψους 17, 3; Ael. Aristid. 28, 103 K.=49 p. 525 D.: π. θεῶν; Philo, Op. M. 63; Jos., Ant. 7, 71, Vi. 167; Just., D. 10, 2; Tat. 15, 2) ὧν μακρὰν καὶ πόρρω πραΰτης B 20:2. S. a for 1Cl 30:3.

ⓒ as comp. of the adv. we have in the text of Lk 24:28 πορρώτερον (Aristot. et al.), and as v.l. πορρωτέρω (X., Pla.; Jos., Bell. 4, 108, Vi. 326.—Thumb 77): πορρ. πορεύεσθαι go farther.—B. 868. DELG s.v. πόρσω. M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

someth. someth. = something

  1. fr. = from

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

prep. prep. = preposition(al)

gen. gen. = genitive

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Thumb Thumb = ATh., Die griechische Sprache im Zeitalter des Hellenismus—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 855
